    text = doc['text']

        if 'construction was completed' in stripped_line.lower() and '2022' in stripped_line:

                    if 'park' in prev_line.lower():

                            'status': 'completed',
                            'et_year': '2022',
                            'topic': 'park'
                        })
                        project_name_found = True
                        break # Found the project name, stop looking further back
            if project_name_found:
                pass


# Convert extracted projects to DataFrame and remove duplicates
projects_df = pd.DataFrame(extracted_projects).drop_duplicates(subset=['Project_Name'])

# Convert funding data to DataFrame
funding_df = pd.DataFrame(funding_data)
funding_df['Amount'] = pd.to_numeric(funding_df['Amount'], errors='coerce')

# Merge dataframes

# If no projects are found, sum will be NaN, return 0.
if pd.isna(total_funding):
    total_funding = 0

print("__RESULT__:")
print(json.dumps(total_funding))

import pandas as pd
import re
import json

funding_data = json.loads(locals()['var_function-call-13812960451374386902'])
civic_docs_file = locals()['var_function-call-8903793629185941567']

    civic_docs_data = json.load(f)

extracted_projects = []

for doc in civic_docs_data:
    text = doc['text']
    lines = text.split('\\n') # Split by literal \n since JSON stores it escaped

    current_project = {}
    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
        stripped_line = line.strip()

        # Heuristic for detecting a new project name
        # A line starting with an uppercase letter, not a bullet, not a common heading/metadata
        # and has at least 2 words.
        is_potential_project_name = bool(re.match(r'^[A-Z][a-zA-Z0-9\s,&-]+(?:\s*\(FEMA Project\)|\s*\(CalOES Project\)|\s*\(CalJPIA Project\))?$', stripped_line)) and \
                                   not stripped_line.startswith(('(cid:', 'To:', 'Prepared by:', 'Approved by:', 'Date prepared:', 'Meeting date:', 'Subject:', 'RECOMMENDED ACTION:', 'DISCUSSION:', 'Updates:', 'Project Schedule:', 'Estimated Schedule:', 'Project Description:', 'Capital Improvement Projects', 'Disaster Recovery Projects', 'Page')) and \
                                   len(stripped_line.split()) >= 2

        if is_potential_project_name:
            # If a new project is detected, process the previous one if it was valid
            if current_project and current_project.get('Project_Name') and current_project.get('status') == 'completed' and current_project.get('et_year') == '2022' and current_project.get('topic') == 'park':
                extracted_projects.append(current_project)
            current_project = {'Project_Name': stripped_line, 'topic': '', 'status': '', 'et_year': ''}
            if 'park' in stripped_line.lower():
                current_project['topic'] = 'park'
            continue # Move to the next line after identifying a new project name

        # If we have a current project, try to extract details
        if current_project.get('Project_Name'):
            # Extract topic - if not already set by project name itself
            if not current_project['topic'] and 'park' in stripped_line.lower():
                current_project['topic'] = 'park'

            # Extract status and end year
            if 'construction was completed' in stripped_line.lower():
                current_project['status'] = 'completed'
                year_match = re.search(r'(\d{4})', stripped_line)
                if year_match:
                    current_project['et_year'] = year_match.group(1)
            elif 'complete construction:' in stripped_line.lower():
                 # Check for 'Complete Construction: Month Year' pattern
                completed_match = re.search(r'Complete Construction:\s*(?:Spring|Summer|Fall|Winter|January|February|March|April|May|June|July|August|September|October|November|December)?\s*(\d{4})', stripped_line, re.IGNORECASE)
                if completed_match:
                    current_project['status'] = 'completed'
                    current_project['et_year'] = completed_match.group(1)

    # After iterating all lines, append the last project if valid
    if current_project and current_project.get('Project_Name') and current_project.get('status') == 'completed' and current_project.get('et_year') == '2022' and current_project.get('topic') == 'park':
        extracted_projects.append(current_project)


# Convert filtered projects to DataFrame and remove duplicates
projects_df = pd.DataFrame(extracted_projects).drop_duplicates(subset=['Project_Name'])

# Convert funding data to DataFrame

funding_df['Amount'] = pd.to_numeric(funding_df['Amount'], errors='coerce')

# Merge dataframes

# If no projects are found, sum will be NaN, return 0.
